steps.title: Spot the signal of restoration
steps.content: The phrase “making the data available again” combined with “stored on outdated technologies” creates a restoration narrative: previously inaccessible data is being returned to a state where it can be used. The word “again” is decisive.

steps.title: Determine what happens to the usefulness
steps.content: ”Renewing” means restoring something to former usefulness—which is precisely what the project does by transferring the data to modern formats. The other choices don't capture this restoration: “persisting” suggests mere continuation, “exceeding” overstates by implying greater usefulness, and “installing” doesn't match the context. That matches choice A — A is correct.
